name: regression
when_to_use: 有一组散点想拟合并画趋势线、标 R² 时用它;覆盖 linear/polynomial/exponential/logarithmic/power 多族(含非线性)拟合。目录里的 simple-statistics 只有单变量一次 linearRegression,本库是它的非线性超集——只算描述统计或纯一次线性用 simple-statistics 即可。
why_valuable: 曲线拟合要解最小二乘(多项式还要解范德蒙系统),AI 徒手估系数只能猜,拟合优度 R² 更是编的。regression 输入 [x,y][] 直接吐方程系数、可读方程串、R² 和 predict(x),让 AI 画拟合曲线并标可信度成为可复算操作。

data_in.note: 严格吃 [x,y][] 点对数组,不是对象行;先按列投影成点对并剔除非有限值。order 只对 polynomial 有意义,precision 控制系数四舍五入位数。

schema.points: [number, number][] —— [x,y] 观测点对;点内含 null 会被忽略,x 有序更稳
schema.order?: number —— 仅 polynomial:多项式阶数,默认 2
schema.precision?: number —— 输出系数四舍五入的有效位数,默认 2

provides._note: headless-primitive:不直接渲染、无 L 级、无 carriers 矩阵;纯 JS 数值层。
provides.outputs: 拟合模型 {equation 系数数组, string 可读方程串, points 拟合点[x,y][], r2 拟合优度, predict(x)→[x,y]}。

api_surface.model: result.equation(系数) · result.string(方程串) · result.r2(R²) · result.points(拟合点[x,y][])
api_surface.predict: result.predict(x) → [x, y](返回点对,非标量 y)

peer.note: 本条只产模型与采样点:对更密的 x 调 result.predict 生成 [x,y][],再叠到散点上画拟合曲线并把 result.r2 标为可信度。

boot.note: predict(x) 返回的是 [x, y] 点对而非标量 y,画平滑曲线时直接把结果推进序列即可。order 仅对 polynomial 生效;点内 null 会被忽略。R² 由库计算,勿自估。

isolation.evidence: Verified vs jsDelivr regression@2.0.1 +esm: single default export (`export{W as default}`), no named exports — `import regression from '…'` then regression.linear/polynomial/exponential/logarithmic/power(points, {order,precision}). README confirms returned model has {equation, string, points, r2, predict}; source (dist/regression.js) defines `const predict = x => ([x, resolveY])` so predict(x) returns an [x,y] pair (not scalar y) and points = data.map(p => predict(p[0])) is [x,y][] — schema/boot pin this. Zero-dependency (package.json dependencies {}). grep of +esm for document.*/window.*/globalThis.*/appendChild/createElement/addEventListener/localStorage = 0 hits — pure JS, shadow-safe.

en.when_to_use: Use it when you have scatter points to fit with a trend line and label with R-squared. It covers linear/polynomial/exponential/logarithmic/power fitting families, including nonlinear fits. The catalog's simple-statistics only has univariate first-order linearRegression, while this library is its nonlinear superset; for descriptive statistics or only simple linear fitting, use simple-statistics.
en.why_valuable: Curve fitting requires least squares (and polynomial fitting solves a Vandermonde system). AI can only guess coefficients by hand and invents goodness-of-fit R-squared. regression accepts [x,y][] and directly returns equation coefficients, a readable equation string, R-squared, and predict(x), making fitted curves and confidence labels reproducible.

data_in.note: Input is strictly an array of [x,y][] point pairs, not object rows. Project columns into point pairs first and remove non-finite values. order matters only for polynomial; precision controls coefficient rounding.

schema.points: [number, number][] -- Observed [x,y] pairs; points containing null are ignored, and ordered x is more stable
schema.order?: number -- polynomial only: polynomial degree; default 2
schema.precision?: number -- Significant digits used to round output coefficients; default 2
